description: Audit nullable values crossing Java, JSON, database, or Android bundle boundaries.
body: Use when Kotlin receives external or Java-originated data. Avoid converting every nullable to default values without caller semantics. Steps: identify input boundary, annotations/schema, expected absent behavior, and public contract; add explicit types or validation; test missing/null cases. Verify compile plus negative path test. Risk boundary is silently changing business meaning of absent data. Use when Kotlin Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: follow the ordered review, edit, and validation sequence; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
